name: Architectural
description: Design patterns that organize larger application boundaries, responsibilities, persistence, presentation, or communication between layers and components.

concepts.name: Model-view-controller
concepts.description: Separates domain data, user interface rendering, and input/control logic into model, view, and controller responsibilities.

concepts.name: Model-view-viewmodel
concepts.description: Separates UI representation from presentation state and behavior through a view model that exposes data and commands to the view.

concepts.name: Repository
concepts.description: Provides a collection-like abstraction over data access, hiding persistence details from domain or application logic.

concepts.name: Unit of work
concepts.description: Tracks changes to objects during a business transaction and coordinates committing or rolling them back as a single unit.

concepts.name: Data access object
concepts.description: Encapsulates persistence operations for a data source or entity, separating storage-specific code from business logic.

concepts.name: Service layer
concepts.description: Defines application operations as services that coordinate domain logic, persistence, transactions, and external integrations.

concepts.name: Front controller
concepts.description: Routes all incoming requests through a single entry point that dispatches to the appropriate handlers or controllers.

concepts.name: Event bus
concepts.description: Provides a shared event dispatch mechanism that lets producers publish events without depending directly on consumers.

concepts.name: Command query responsibility segregation
concepts.description: Separates operations that change state from operations that read state, often using different models or handlers for commands and queries.
